parsed.summary: Cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff adds 6600+ lines across 84 files, including new retry/error-dispatch infrastructure and a deleted library module. Three material gaps remain: (1) tracked-jobs.mjs is 78 lines of new stateful job-tracking logic with zero test coverage — the only new library module without tests; (2) process.mjs was deleted, creating a latent MODULE_NOT_FOUND crash risk if any importer survived the Bundle F purge; (3) glm-client.mjs absorbed 155 lines of retry/error-dispatch integration without a dedicated test file, leaving the most critical API-surface error chain only indirectly exercised. Any one of these can produce silent failures or hard crashes under real workloads.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs: new stateful job-tracking module with no test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs adds 78 lines of job state management — inherently stateful, concurrency-sensitive, and directly affecting data integrity. Every other new library module (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, fs.mjs, state.mjs, preset-config.mjs, render.mjs, args.mjs) has a dedicated test file. tracked-jobs.mjs does not. No tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs appears anywhere in the diff. Race conditions in concurrent job creation, stale-state accumulation, or cleanup failures on cancellation/timeout will ship undetected. Job tracking errors can cause lost work, duplicated submissions, or orphaned state — all expensive to recover from and hard to diagnose without tests defining expected behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: concurrent job creation races, all state transitions (created→running→done/failed/cancelled), cleanup on timeout, and stale-state recovery. Block release until this coverage exists.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs: core client retry integration untested at unit level
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs received 155 lines of changes integrating the new retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs modules. No dedicated test file exists (no tests/glm-client.test.mjs in the diff). This is the primary API surface — the full error-handling chain (glm-client → retry.mjs → bigmodel-errors.mjs) is only exercised indirectly through render tests and result-propagation tests. Incorrect error classification, retry on non-idempotent operations, or swallowed errors in this chain would silently degrade or corrupt every downstream command's behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs with mocked HTTP responses covering: retry on transient errors, non-retry on permanent errors, error-classification propagation from bigmodel-errors.mjs, timeout behavior, and abort-signal cancellation. At minimum, add integration tests that exercise the full retry→error-dispatch→client chain end-to-end.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — latent dangling-import crash risk
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was removed, apparently during 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge'. If any surviving module still imports from './lib/process.mjs' or 'scripts/lib/process.mjs', the import will fail at runtime with MODULE_NOT_FOUND — a hard crash with no fallback. The purge commit references fixing ReferenceErrors, suggesting removal was reactive. The check-imports.mjs script was modified (5 lines changed) but there is no evidence it validates that imports resolve to existing files rather than just checking import style. Without a systematic grep, this risk remains open across 6 subsequent versions.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/` and verify zero surviving imports. If check-imports.mjs does not verify import targets exist, add that check to prevent future regressions of this class.

next_steps: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs — this is the strongest blocking gap (stateful module, zero tests, data-integrity impact).
Verify no surviving imports reference scripts/lib/process.mjs: `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/`.
Add unit or integration tests for glm-client.mjs covering the retry→error-dispatch→client chain.
Confirm check-imports.mjs validates that import targets resolve to existing files, preventing future dangling-import regressions.
